title: Child Protection Specialist Centre (CPSC)
text:
Child Protection Specialist Centre (CPSC) - SupportGoWhere
Child Protection Specialist Centre (CPSC)
Share link
Serves as specialists on child protection matters to provide community-based support to children who face abuse or neglect at home.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Free
Referral required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

Child Protection Specialist Centres (CPSCs) provide child-centric, family-focused and community-based support to children who face abuse or neglect at home.

Support provided includes:

Safety planning

Casework intervention

Counselling to address the risks posed to the children and young persons

Practical and other relevant services to the children and young persons and their families

Who is this for?

Children and young persons who face abuse or neglect at home.

What to expect?

After receiving the referral, the social worker from CPSC will contact the referring person to find out more about the presenting abuse or neglect concerns.

The CPSC will then inform on whether the referral is assessed to be suitable for CPSC to provide the intervention.

If assessed to not be suitable, CPSC will advise on the other possible services or contact points.

How to apply?

Referral is required.

Contact CPSC via phone call or email to enquire on the referral process.

Information last updated on 29 Sep 2025
